rulings.Case Title: COLBY LAM VS. ANDREW KENNY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication On Plaintiffs First, Second, Third, And Fifth Causes Of Action

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY LA ROCCA VS. JORGE ALVARADO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 22, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT JORGE ALVARADO, JORGE ALVARADO, DAMARIS ROMERO, LAURA MAGANA, GUSTAVO CUBILLO, WILLIAM CUBILLO, JENNY CUBILLO MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES DENIED. Moving party failed to establish that it is entitled to attorney's fees in this action. Moving papers cite no authority (beyond a passing reference to Cal. Civ. Code 1717) and provide no analysis as to the entitlement to fees under the terms of the lease. Reply papers cite several cases, none of which are unlawful detainer cases, let alone Owner/Relative Move-In Cases. Furthermore, the Lease containing the purported attorney's fees is not properly authenticated in the moving papers. Finally, moving party fails to address unlawful detainer authorities cited in opposition, cited for the proposition that "if an unlawful detainer action is based on an alleged breach of the lease during an unexpired term (e.g., nonpayment of rent, improper use of the premises), then it is an action sounding in contract." No argument is provided addressing how the action at bar meets this test and/or why this test is not applicable. The Court further notes that the attorney's fees clause is narrowly applicable to enforcement of the Agreement. No argument is provided as to how this action is an action to enforce any term of the Lease. =(501/CFH)
